channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UN suspends its humanitarian work in Houthi rebels' stronghold in Yemen after more staff detentions
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it suspended its humanitarian operations in the stronghold of Yemen’s Houthi rebels after they detained eight more U.N. staffers, affecting the global response to one of the world’s worst humanitarian disasters. In a statement, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Venezuela sends 2 planes to US to return migrants, signaling a potential improvement in relations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Two Venezuelan planes flew to the United States on Monday and returned home with deported Venezuelans, signaling a possible improvement in relations between longtime diplomatic adversaries and a victory for President Donald Trump in his efforts to get more countries to take the
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Labor unions sue to block DOGE access to sensitive information at US agencies
item.description: A coalition of labor unions filed a lawsuit Monday asking a federal court to stop Elon Musk’s team from accessing private data at the Education Department, the Treasury Department and the Office of Personnel Management.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Confusion after emergency alert sent to phones in San Francisco
item.description: (KRON) -- Some cell phone users in San Francisco received an emergency alert Monday afternoon. Phones sounded the emergency alert tone after a notification said that an endangered adult was missing.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: American tourists attacked by shark at luxe Bahamas resort area
item.description: Police are investigating after two female American tourists were apparently involved in a shark attack at a popular Bahamas resort over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hoarder arrested for extreme animal cruelty, as authorities discover 10 dead pets, rescue many more: police
item.description: A Long Island woman was arrested and charged with animal cruelty and other charges after a search warrant on her residence revealed unlivable conditions for dozens of animals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles family featured in Super Bowl commercial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Taco Bell marked its return to the Super Bowl with a commercial that featured nearly 400 fans, including a couple from Los Angeles and their newborn baby.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Second sinkhole opens up on I-80 in New Jersey, shutting down roadway
item.description: A 40-foot by 40-foot hole opened up at the same exit on Dec. 26, caused by the collapse of an abandoned mineshaft underneath, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Alexa Farrell, NewsNation

item.title: Biggest storm of the year is about to hit California. Here's how much rain to expect
item.description: “Looks like Wednesday, Thursday, Friday we’ll have at least on-again-off-again rain showers. It will accumulate, so we do get some decent rainfall numbers,” meteorologist Henry DiCarlo said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: So long, penny! Trump orders US to ditch 1-cent coin after decades of complaints
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — The lowly penny, the forgotten mainstay of coin jars and car cupholders everywhere, may soon be no more. President Donald Trump announced Sunday he’s ordered his administration to cease production of the 1-cent coin, whose buying power is long past its prime.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge finds Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending
item.description: A federal judge found Monday that the Trump administration hasn’t fully followed his order to unfreeze federal spending and told the White House to release all the money.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has unleashed chaos by distraction upon the international community. That's no accident
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Saudis are furious. The Danes are scrambling. Colombia has backed down. Mexico and Canada stand in a purgatory between tariff wars with the US and … not. China has retaliated, launching a trade war between the economic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Court grants request to block detained Venezuelan immigrants from being sent to Guantanamo
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A federal court on Sunday blocked the Trump administration from sending three Venezuelan immigrants held in New Mexico to Guantanamo Bay Naval Base in Cuba as part of the president's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PlayStation Network back up after dealing with technical issues since Friday
item.description: (KRON) -- The PlayStation Network announced late Saturday afternoon that "all services are up and running" after it experienced issues across its platforms for almost 24 hours. Users were first alerted of service issues at 4 p.m. on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: A tax credit worth up to $7,830 often goes unclaimed: See who qualifies for the EITC
item.description: The IRS estimates that a fifth of eligible taxpayers fail to take advantage of the tax break each year.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: The US stopped allowing passport gender marker changes. Here are some of the people affected
item.description: The day after President Donald Trump returned to office, Lisa Suhay took her 21-year-old daughter, Mellow, to a passport office in Norfolk, Virginia, where they live. Getting a passport for Mellow, who is transgender, was urgent.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge early Saturday blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans. U.S. District Judge Paul A.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Revamped Superdome is set to host its 8th Super Bowl
item.description: On Sunday, the hulking, 70,000-seat, triple-decker stadium — an unmistakable figure in the New Orleans skyline with its champagne-colored metallic sheen and hourglass shape — will host a Super Bowl for the eighth time.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: A$AP Rocky's friend testifies that the rapper fired a prop gun, not a real firearm, in 2021 shooting
item.description: A$AP Rocky's longtime friend and collaborator testified at his Los Angeles trial Friday that the hip-hop star fired a starter pistol that he carried as a prop during a scuffle with a former friend on a Hollywood street in 2021.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: A mother and daughter were killed in tornadoes that struck eastern Tennessee
item.description: WARTBURG, Tenn. (AP) — Two tornadoes moved through eastern Tennessee, leaving a mother and daughter dead and injuring three other people, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Louisiana woman arrested following death of Kansas City reporter covering Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Louisiana woman has been arrested after she was allegedly caught using the reporter's credit cards in the New Orleans area.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, WGN 9

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama puts man to death for a 1991 murder in the nation's fourth execution using nitrogen gas
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An man convicted of murdering a woman after breaking into her apartment as she slept was put to death Thursday evening in Alabama in the nation’s fourth execution using nitrogen gas. Demetrius Frazier, 52, was pronounced dead at 6:36 p.m.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 13 states to sue over DOGE access to government payment systems containing personal data
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in several states vowed Thursday to file a lawsuit to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing federal payment systems containing Americans' sensitive personal information.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4
